papers.description: Multiple choice objective test which will last 1 hour and carry 60 marks.
Essay test in three sections (Sections A, B, and C) with rubrics written in Hausa. Will take 2 hours and carry 100 marks.
papers.instructions: Candidates will be required to answer six questions: three from Section A, two from Section B, and one from Section C, using official Hausa orthography and appropriate metalanguage.

subsections.title: Composition and Letter Writing
topics: Narrative (Insha'i Na Labari)
Descriptive (Insha'i Na Siffance)
Argumentative (Insha'i Na Muhawara)
Expository (Insha'i Na Bayani)
Dialogue (Muhawara ta kai tsaye)
Proverbial (Karin Magana)
Letter Writing (Rubutun Wasika)

subsections.title: Sound System (Tsarin Sauti)
topics: Consonants, Vowels and Syllable Structure (Furucin bakake da wasula da kirar gaba)
Structure of Syllable (Tsarin gaba)
Types of Syllable (ire-iren gaba): open syllable (budaddiyar gaba), close syllable (rufaffiyar gaba)
Vowel production (furucin Wasula): vowel length, diphthongs
Tone (Karin Sauti): High tone, Low tone, Falling tone

subsections.title: Grammar (Nahawu)
topics: Word Structure And Word Formation (Ginin kalmomi): Root, Affix, Prefix, Infix, Suffix
Word Classes (Azuzuwan kalmomi): Noun, Verb, Adjective, Adverb, Pronoun, Conjunction, Specifier, Quantifier, Demonstrative, Ideophone, Diminutive, Stabilizer, Referential
Grammatical Categories (Rukunonin Nahawu): Tense and Aspect, Negative, Gender, Number
The structure and Types of Sentences (Tsari da ire-iren jumloli): Subject and predicate, Simple sentence, Complex sentence, Compound sentence

sections.description: Oral and Written Literature. Candidates attempt two questions: one from Oral Literature and one from Written Literature.

genres: Prose (Tatsuniya da tarihihi da Karin Magana da Ka-Cinci-Ka-Cinci)

sections.description: Customs and Institutions. Two questions set, one to be answered.
topics: Greeting (Gaisuwar ban girma, Gaisuwar taya murna, Gaisuwar lokatai, Jaje, Ta'aziyya)
Tarbiyya (Hanyoyin tarbiyya, Girmama iyaye, Girmama na gaba, Girmama tsara, Da'a ga hukuma)
Hospitality (karimci): Girmama bako, Kyauta, Ciyarwa, Sadaka
Family (Iyali): Al'adun Aure, Haihuwa, Radin Suna, Reno, Yaye, Kaciya, Zumunci, Al'adun Mutuwa
Traditional Beliefs and Practices (camfi, bori, duba, tsibbu, maita, fatalwa, d.s.)
Traditional Occupations (Noma, Kira, Saka, Jima, Wanzanci, Kitso, Sassaka, Kiwo, Dukanci, Fawa, Su, Rini, Kasuwanci, Dillanci)
Traditional Medicine (Magungunan gargajiya)
Traditional Authority and office holding (sarautun gargajiya)
Forms of Traditional Recreation (Wasannin dandali): Dambe, Kokawa, Kalankuwa, Shanci, Sukuwa, Hawan kaho

language_sound_system_and_grammar_refere.title: Exam Focus Hausa Language
language_sound_system_and_grammar_refere.author: Sani, M. A. Z. et al.

reference_texts.title: Harshe Da Adabin Hausa A Kamalle
reference_texts.author: Ismaila, J. and Tanimu, M. Y.

reference_texts.title: Labaru Na Da Da Na Yanzu
